reasoning: F1 is officially nominated for Best Film Editing, validating part of its path to victory. Furthermore, it secured a Best Picture nomination and importantly won the Critics Choice Award for Best Editing in early January. While the overarching Best Picture frontrunner, "One Battle After Another", is heavily favored by general Gold Derby experts (who often default Best Editing to the Best Picture winner), specific prominent prognosticators such as The Hollywood Reporter's Scott Feinberg and Awards Daily have F1 ranked as their #1 predicted winner for Editing. Historically, high-octane technical films with visible cutting (like Ford v Ferrari and Mad Max: Fury Road) frequently win Best Editing despite not winning Best Picture. Taking into account its Critics Choice win and strong advocate base vs the general momentum of "One Battle After Another", F1 has roughly a 35% chance to win.
